scenarios.title: Section 44AD — Small Trader with Mandatory Bank Balance Disclosure
scenarios.fy202526Highlight: Mandatory cash and bank balance disclosure in Schedule BP (new for FY 2025-26)

incomes.note: Since digital receipts > 60%, enhanced limit of ₹3Cr applies. Turnover ₹18L << limit.
incomes.presumptiveIncomeRate: 8% on cash portion + 6% on digital portion
incomes.note2: Since digital > 60%, can use 6% on digital. Alternatively, declare 8% on full turnover (simpler).

schedulesToFill.notes: Section 44AD presumptive income + MANDATORY cash/bank balance

stepByStepFilling.action: Enter turnover, declare presumptive income (minimum 8% or 6%), and MANDATORY cash/bank balance.

sampleValues.note: Declaring ₹1,50,000 is 8.33% of turnover — above the 8% minimum. Valid.

totalFinancialParticulars.note: MANDATORY from FY 2025-26. Verify from bank passbooks/statements.

redFlagsInScenario: Cash balance ₹25,000 must match actual physical cash — discrepancy triggers 69A proceedings
Bank balance ₹2,25,000 must match closing balances in bank passbooks as on March 31, 2026
GST turnover in GSTR-9 must reconcile with ITR turnover of ₹18 lakh
commonMistakesToAvoid: Forgetting to fill cash/bank balance fields — they are mandatory from FY 2025-26
Entering zero bank balance when accounts have funds — AIS will show actual data
Declaring income below 8% without getting tax audit done

scenarios.title: Section 44ADA — Doctor with 2 House Properties (NEW FY 2025-26 Feature)
scenarios.fy202526Highlight: First time filing ITR-4 with 2 house properties (expanded from 1 in FY 2024-25)

incomes.note: ₹48 lakh gross receipts (below ₹75L limit for 44ADA with high digital proportion)
incomes.house2_type: Let-Out (flat in Pune rented to tenant)

schedulesToFill.notes: BOTH properties — new for FY 2025-26 in ITR-4
Advance tax paid (₹24L income requires advance tax)

sampleValues.note: 50% of ₹48L = ₹24L. Dr. Krishnan declares exactly the minimum.
Under NEW regime, home loan interest on self-occupied property is NOT deductible. Income = Nil.

stepByStepFilling.section: Schedule HP — Property 2 (Let-Out) — NEW for FY 2025-26 in ITR-4

sampleValues.note: Net HP income from let-out = ₹1,60,000 - ₹48,000 - ₹1,20,000 = -₹8,000 (loss). Under new regime, HP loss from let-out can be set off against HP income but NOT against other income in some interpretations — verify with current CBDT guidance.
44ADA taxpayers CAN pay all advance tax in one installment by March 15. Dr. Krishnan chose to pay quarterly.

redFlagsInScenario: Bank balance ₹11.7L with declared income ₹24L — department will check if income is consistent
GST returns (if registered) must show ₹48L gross receipts — match with ITR
Second property in Pune — stamp duty registration shows ownership; non-disclosure not possible
commonMistakesToAvoid: Filing ITR-3 unnecessarily when ITR-4 now accepts 2 house properties — ITR-4 is simpler
Claiming home loan interest for self-occupied under new regime
Advance tax default: if ₹3.14L tax was expected but only ₹2.9L paid in advance, interest u/s 234B applies on ₹24K

scenarios.title: Section 44AD + LTCG u/s 112A Within ₹1.25L — New Feature (FY 2025-26)
scenarios.fy202526Highlight: For the first time, LTCG u/s 112A (within ₹1.25L) can be reported directly in ITR-4

incomes.ltcg_112A_soldShares: Redeemed equity mutual fund units held 18 months
incomes.note: LTCG of ₹70,000 < ₹1,25,000 exemption limit. Full LTCG is exempt. Zero capital gains tax.

schedulesToFill.notes: LTCG 112A — allowed in ITR-4 for FY 2025-26 (NEW)

stepByStepFilling.section: Schedule CG — LTCG u/s 112A (NEW in ITR-4 for FY 2025-26)
stepByStepFilling.action: Report LTCG from equity MF units. Total LTCG ₹70,000 is within ₹1.25L exemption. Zero taxable CG.

sampleValues.typeOfGain: LTCG u/s 112A — Equity Oriented MF Units
sampleValues.note: ₹70,000 < ₹1,25,000 — fully exempt. No tax on LTCG.

calculation.note: Income < ₹4L under new regime → Zero tax.

redFlagsInScenario: If LTCG from equity MF redemptions had been ₹1.30L (above ₹1.25L), must switch to ITR-2/ITR-3
Broker/AMC reports MF redemptions to AIS — undisclosed redemptions create mismatch
commonMistakesToAvoid: Not filling Schedule CG even for exempt LTCG — exempt LTCG must be disclosed
Confusing STCG (held <12 months) with LTCG — STCG on equity MF is taxed at 20% and cannot be filed in ITR-4
Thinking grandfathering (FMV as on Jan 31, 2018) applies — only for pre-Feb 2018 acquisitions

scenarios.title: Section 44AE — Goods Transport Operator (5 Trucks)

incomes.computationNote: Heavy vehicle: ₹1,000 per tonne per month. LCV/Mini truck: ₹7,500 per vehicle per month.

calculation.note: Income > ₹5L → no rebate under old regime.

redFlagsInScenario: Vehicle registration certificates must show ownership duration — partial year (like Vehicle 5 with 6 months) must be correctly computed
If more than 10 vehicles owned at any time during FY, 44AE does not apply — must use regular books
commonMistakesToAvoid: Computing income per month for heavy vehicles without using gross vehicle weight
Including hired/rented vehicles in the count — 44AE covers only OWNED vehicles

scenarios.title: HUF with Section 44AD Presumptive Business Income
scenarios.importantNote: HUF is a separate tax entity from the Karta and members. The HUF files its own ITR-4. Income earned by the HUF in HUF's name (from HUF property, HUF business) is taxed in HUF's hands. Members then receive their share from HUF income — which is exempt u/s 10(2).

calculation.note: Income < ₹4L — zero tax under new regime

redFlagsInScenario: Members' incomes are separate — HUF income and member income must not be clubbed
If Karta pays himself remuneration from HUF, that is taxable in Karta's hands as salary — not deductible from HUF income in ITR-4
commonMistakesToAvoid: Confusing HUF income with Karta's personal income
44ADA is NOT available for HUF — only 44AD and 44AE are available for HUF
HUF cannot use ITR-1 — must use at minimum ITR-2; ITR-4 if presumptive income

scenarios.title: Compliance Scenario — Schedule BP Bank Balance Disclosure (FY 2025-26 Mandatory)
scenarios.fy202526Highlight: Detailed walkthrough of the mandatory cash and bank balance disclosure process
scenarios.purpose: This scenario explains HOW to correctly gather and disclose the mandatory cash and bank balance in Schedule BP for FY 2025-26. This is a procedural compliance scenario rather than a tax computation example.
complianceSteps: Step 1: Download bank account statements for ALL accounts as of March 31, 2026
Step 2: Note the CLOSING BALANCE (not opening balance) from each statement
Step 3: Sum all closing balances = aggregate bank balance to disclose
Step 4: Count all physical cash (at shop, at home, in transit) as on March 31 evening
Step 5: Include all accounts — even dormant ones with balance
Step 6: Do NOT exclude any account — include FD accounts if they show a 'balance' in passbook
Step 7: Enter both figures in Schedule BP: Cash in Hand and Bank Balance
whatToInclude: Cash credit/overdraft accounts with positive balance
NRO accounts if applicable (resident returning from abroad)
whatNotToInclude: Fixed deposits (shown separately as investments if material)
redFlagsInScenario: AIS (Annual Information Statement) now shows bank account balances reported by banks — discrepancy between AIS data and ITR disclosure will trigger notice
Declaring significantly lower bank balance than what AIS shows → scrutiny u/s 143(2)
Having high bank balance (e.g., ₹50L) relative to declared income (e.g., ₹5L) → Section 69A proceedings
commonMistakesToAvoid: Omitting a dormant account with small balance — include all
Not including personal savings accounts — all accounts of the individual must be included
Reporting bank balance from a date other than March 31, 2026
Leaving fields blank — they are mandatory and cannot be skipped in the form utility

scenarios.title: When to Switch from ITR-4 to ITR-3 — Disqualification Scenario
scenarios.purpose: This scenario identifies conditions that DISQUALIFY ITR-4 and require switching to ITR-3. Useful for self-assessment before form selection.

disqualifyingConditions.details: 44AD: Turnover > ₹3 Crore (or > ₹2Cr if cash transactions > 5%). 44ADA: Gross receipts > ₹75L (or > ₹50L with high cash). 44AE: More than 10 vehicles owned.
disqualifyingConditions.consequence: Must use ITR-3 with full books of accounts and tax audit if applicable.
disqualifyingConditions.example: Ramu Traders had ₹3.5 Crore turnover in FY 2025-26. Cannot use ITR-4.

disqualifyingConditions.condition: Declaring income below minimum presumptive rate (44AD: <8% or <6%; 44ADA: <50%)
disqualifyingConditions.details: If actual profits are lower than the presumptive rate and taxpayer wants to declare actual profits, must maintain regular books and get audit under Section 44AB.
disqualifyingConditions.consequence: Switch to ITR-3. Maintain books for that year. Tax audit u/s 44AB required if turnover > ₹1Cr.
disqualifyingConditions.example: Ramesh's actual profit = 5% of turnover = ₹1,50,000 against minimum required 8% = ₹2,40,000. He must maintain books and file ITR-3.

disqualifyingConditions.condition: Opted out of 44AD in any of previous 5 FYs
disqualifyingConditions.details: Section 44AD(4): If taxpayer did not declare income under 44AD for any FY after having once opted in, they cannot use 44AD for 5 years.
disqualifyingConditions.consequence: Must file ITR-3 for the prohibited 5-year period.
disqualifyingConditions.example: Kishore used 44AD in FY 2020-21 but not in FY 2021-22 (filed ITR-3 with books). He cannot use 44AD again until FY 2026-27.

disqualifyingConditions.details: More than 2 house properties disqualify ITR-4. Even if all properties are self-occupied.
disqualifyingConditions.example: Preeti owns a flat in Delhi, house in Lucknow, and inherited property in Varanasi (3 properties). Cannot use ITR-4.

disqualifyingConditions.details: Any STCG, or LTCG on property or other assets, or LTCG u/s 112A exceeding ₹1.25L, or capital losses being carried forward.
disqualifyingConditions.consequence: Switch to ITR-2 (if no business income) or ITR-3 (if business income).
disqualifyingConditions.example: Arjun sold ancestral land (LTCG on immovable property). Even though he has 44AD income, must use ITR-3.

disqualifyingConditions.details: Being a director in any company — private, public, or foreign — disqualifies ITR-4.
disqualifyingConditions.example: A shopkeeper who is also a nominee director in a family company cannot use ITR-4.

scenarios.switchingProcedure: If a mid-year event disqualifies ITR-4 (e.g., crossing turnover limit), the taxpayer should: (1) Maintain books of accounts for the year, (2) Get tax audit if applicable, (3) File ITR-3 with complete financial statements.
